etymology.description: 門(門) (mén, "door") + abbreviated 材(材) (cái, "material; timber") = a door with material blocking it -> shut; close
etymology.interpretation: The character depicts a door being obstructed by some material, symbolizing the act of shutting or closing it.
etymology.simplification: The traditional form 閉(閉) was simplified by reducing 門(門) (mén, "door") to 门(门) in the simplified character 闭(闭).

examples.chinese: 请把门闭上(請把門閉上)。
他闭了一只眼睛(他閉了一隻眼睛)，假装在睡觉(假裝在睡覺)。
examples.pinyin: Tā bìle yī zhī yǎnjīng, jiǎzhuāng zài shuìjiào.
examples.english: He closed one eye, pretending to sleep.

examples.chinese: 这个窗户很难闭上(這個窗戶很難閉上)，你需要用点力气(你需要用點力氣)。
examples.pinyin: Zhège chuānghù hěn nán bì shàng, nǐ xūyào yòng diǎn lìqi.
examples.english: This window is hard to close; you need to use some force.
